drama: S01E06 · The One with the Butt

lines.text: [Scene: A Theater, the gang is in the audience wating for a play of Joey's to start.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (reading the program) Ooh! Look! Look! Look! Look, there's Joey's picture! This is so exciting!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: You can always spot someone who's never seen one of his plays before. Notice, no fear, no sense of impending doom...
lines.korean: 이 친구 연극을 한 번도 안 본 사람은 딱 티가 나. 봐, 두려움도 없고, 닥쳐올 재앙에 대한 감도 없잖아…
lines.explanation: spot = (단번에) 알아채다/포착하다. notice = 봐봐. impending doom = 임박한 재앙/불길한 예감(impending = 곧 닥칠).

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: The exclamation point in the title scares me. (Gesturing) Y'know, it's not just Freud, it's Freud!
lines.korean: 제목에 붙은 느낌표가 무서워. 그냥 '프로이트'가 아니라 '프로이트!'잖아.
lines.explanation: exclamation point = 느낌표. 연극 제목이 'Freud!'(정신분석학 창시자 지크문트 프로이트)인데, 느낌표 때문에 더 부담스럽다는 농담.

lines.text: (The lights dim.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh, shhh, shh. Magic is about to happen.

lines.text: (The lights go up on the stage, Joey, as Freud, is talking to a female patient.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Vell, Eva, ve've done some excellent vork here, and I vould have to say, your pwoblem is qviiite clear. (He goes into a song and dance number.)

lines.text: All you want is a dingle, What you envy's a schwang, A thing through which you can tinkle, Or play with, or simply let hang...
Opening Credits
[Scene: The Theater, the play has ended and everyone is applauding. As soon as the cast leaves, the gang all groan and sit down heavily.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: God. I feel violated.
lines.korean: 세상에. 뭔가 유린당한 기분이야.
lines.explanation: violated = (인격·사생활이) 침해당한/유린당한 듯한 (형편없는 연극을 보고 난 느낌).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Did anybody else feel they just wanted to peel the skin off their body, to have something else to do?
lines.korean: 혹시 나 말고도, 뭐라도 다른 할 일이 생기게 자기 몸 살갗이라도 벗겨내고 싶었던 사람 없어?
lines.explanation: peel the skin off = 살갗을 벗겨내다(peel ~ off = ~을 벗겨내다). 'to have something else to do' = 뭐라도 다른 할 일이 생기도록 — 연극이 너무 지루해서 차라리 딴짓거리라도 만들고 싶었다는 과장.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (staring at a woman across the room) Ross, ten o'clock.
lines.korean: 로스, 10시 방향.
lines.explanation: ten o'clock = 시계 방향으로 위치를 알려주는 표현(정면이 12시, 왼쪽 앞이 10시 방향). 누가 어디 있는지 가리킬 때 씀.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Is it? Feels like two.
lines.korean: 그래? 두 시간 같은데.
lines.explanation: 챈들러가 'ten o'clock(10시 방향)'이라 했는데 로스가 시각으로 알아듣고 '(지루해서) 두 시간 같다'고 받는 말장난.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: No, ten o'clock.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (sighs and gestures to explain) There's a beautiful woman at eight, nine, ten o'clock!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh. Hel-lo!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: She's amazing! She makes the women that I dream about look like short, fat, bald men!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Well, go over to her! She's not with anyone.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh yeah, and what would my opening line be? 'Excuse me. Blarrglarrghh.'
lines.korean: 제발. 첫마디를 뭐라고 해? '실례합니다. 으어버버.' 이러라고?
lines.explanation: opening line = (작업 걸 때) 첫마디/말 거는 멘트.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, c'mon. She's a person, you can do it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh please, could she be more out of my league? Ross, back me up here.
lines.korean: 제발. 저 여자가 나한테 과분해도 더 과분할 수 있을까? 로스, 좀 거들어줘.
lines.explanation: out of one's league = (상대가) 나한텐 과분한/넘사벽인. back (someone) up = ~를 거들어주다/편들어주다(phrasal).
examples: She's way out of my league.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: He could never get a woman like that in a million years.
lines.korean: 쟤는 백만 년이 지나도 저런 여자 절대 못 사귀어.
lines.explanation: in a million years = 백만 년이 지나도(=절대로). 거들어달라니까 '절대 못 한다'고 맞장구쳐 버리는 농담.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Thank you, buddy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, oh, but y'know, you always see these really beautiful women with these really nothing guys, you could be one of those guys.
lines.korean: 근데 진짜 예쁜 여자들이 진짜 별 볼 일 없는 남자랑 있는 거 자주 보잖아, 너도 그런 남자 될 수 있어.
lines.explanation: nothing guy = 별 볼 일 없는/내세울 것 없는 남자(nothing이 형용사처럼 쓰임).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You could do that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Y'think?

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh God, I can't believe I'm even considering this... I'm very very aware of my tongue...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: C'mon! C'mon!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Here goes. (He walks over to her but just stands there.)

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: ...Yes?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hi.... um... okay, next word... would be... Chandler! Chandler is my name, and, uh...(He clears his throat noisily)...hi.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Yes, you said that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yes, yes I did, but what I didn't say was what I was about to say, what I wanted to say was, uh... would you like to go out with me sometime, thankyou, goodnight. (He walks back to the others but she calls him back.)

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Chandler?

lines.text: (Joey enters from behind a curtain. The others all talk at once.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Hey! You're in a play! I didn't know you could dance! You had a beard!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Whadja think?

lines.text: (Pause)

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: ...Hey! You're in a play! I didn't know you could dance! You had a beard!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: C'mon, you guys, it wasn't that bad. I was the lead. It was better than that thing I did with the trolls, at least you got to see my head.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: (admitting) Saw your head. Saw your head.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (running back) She said yes!! She said yes!! (To Joey) Awful play, man. Whoah. (To All) Her name's Aurora, and she's Italian, and she pronounces my name 'Chand-lrr'. 'Chand-lrr'. I think I like it better that way. (To Joey) Oh, listen, the usher gave me this to give to you. (He fishes a card out of his pocket.)
lines.korean: 걔가 그러자고 했어!! 이름은 오로라고, 이탈리아 사람인데, 내 이름을 '챈들르'라고 발음해… 아, 안내원이 너 주라고 이거 줬어.
lines.explanation: usher = (극장·결혼식 등의) 좌석 안내원. (오로라가 챈들러 이름을 이탈리아식으로 굴려 발음하는 것) / I like it better that way = 그렇게 부르는 게 더 맘에 들어. like ~ better = 더 좋아하다, that way = 그런 식으로. 이때 목적어 it이 꼭 있어야 해서 'I like that way'는 틀리고 'I like it (better) that way'가 맞다.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: What is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: The Estelle Leonard Talent Agency. Wow, an agency left me its card! Maybe they wanna sign me!
lines.korean: 에스텔 레너드 탤런트 에이전시. 와, 에이전시가 나한테 명함을 줬어! 날 계약하려는 건가!
lines.explanation: agency = (배우·모델) 에이전시/기획사. sign (someone) = (계약을 맺어) ~를 영입하다/계약하다.
examples: The label wants to sign her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Based on this play? ...Based on this play!

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, everyone else is there as Chandler enters.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hey, kids.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (reading Monica's palm) No, 'cause this line is passion, and this is... just a line.
lines.korean: 아니, 이 선은 열정 선이고, 이건… 그냥 선이야.
lines.explanation: (reading the palm) 손금을 봐주는 장면. passion line처럼 손금 줄에 의미를 붙이는데, 피비가 하나는 '그냥 선'이라고 시큰둥하게 받아침.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Well, I can't believe I've been here almost seven seconds and you haven't asked me how my date went.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, right, right. How was your date, 'Chand-lrr'?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: It was unbelievable. I-I've never met anyone like her. She's had the most amazing life! She was in the Israeli army...

lines.text: (A flashback of Aurora and Chandler on their date in Central Perk is denoted by italics.)

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: ...Luckily none of the bullets hit the engine block. So, we made it to the border, but just barely, and I- ...I've been talking about myself all night long, I'm sorry. What about you? Tell me one of your stories.
lines.korean: …다행히 총알이 엔진 본체엔 안 맞아서, 간신히 국경까지 갔어, 정말 아슬아슬하게. 그런데- …내가 밤새 내 얘기만 했네, 미안. 넌 어때? 네 얘기 하나 해봐.
lines.explanation: just barely = 가까스로/간신히. engine block = 엔진 본체(블록).

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Alright. Once I got on the subway, right, and it was at night, and I rode it all the way to Brooklyn... just for the hell of it.
lines.korean: 그래. 한번은 밤에 지하철을 탔는데, 그냥 재미로 브루클린까지 끝까지 갔었어.
lines.explanation: just for the hell of it = 그냥 재미로/별 이유 없이. (오로라의 파란만장한 일화에 비해 시시한 자기 얘기를 꺼내는 챈들러)
examples: I learned Korean just for the hell of it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: We talked 'til like two. It was this perfect evening... more or less.
lines.korean: 우린 두 시쯤까지 얘기했어. 완벽한 저녁이었지… 뭐, 대체로.
lines.explanation: more or less = 대체로/그럭저럭/얼추.
examples: The work is more or less done.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: ...All of a sudden we realised we were in Yammon.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, I'm sorry, so 'we' is?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: 'We' would be me and Rick.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Who's Rick?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Who's Rick?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: My husband.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Ooooohhh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, so you're divorced?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, I'm sorry, then you're widowed?...Hopefully?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: No, I'm still married.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: So tell me, how do- how do you think your husband would feel about you sitting here with me?...Sliding your foot so far up my pant leg you can count the change in my pocket?
lines.korean: 그래서 말인데, 남편은 당신이 여기 나랑 이러고 앉아 있는 거 어떻게 생각할까?…내 바짓가랑이 안으로 발을 하도 깊이 밀어 올려서 내 주머니 잔돈까지 셀 정도로 말이야.
lines.explanation: pant leg = 바짓가랑이(바지 한쪽 다리 부분). so far up ~ (that) you can count ~ = 하도 위까지 깊이 밀어 올려서 ~할 정도로(so ... that 강조 구문, far up = 위로 한참 깊이). 유부녀이면서 테이블 밑으로 발을 더듬는 오로라를 비꼬는 과장.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Don't worry. I imagine he'd be okay with you because really, he's okay with Ethan.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Ethan? There's, there's an Ethan?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Mmmm... Ethan is my... boyfriend.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: So explain something to me here, uh, what kind of a relationship do you imagine us having if you already have a husband and a boyfriend?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: I suppose mainly sexual.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh. I'm sorry it didn't work out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: What 'not work out'? I'm seeing her again on Thursday. Didn't you listen to the story?
lines.korean: '잘 안 됐다'니 무슨 소리야? 목요일에 또 만나기로 했는데. 얘기 안 들었어?
lines.explanation: work out = (일·관계가) 잘 풀리다/성사되다(phrasal).
examples: I hope things work out between you two.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Didn't you listen to the story? I mean, this is twisted! How could you get involved with a woman like this?
lines.korean: 넌 얘기 안 들었어? 이건 완전 변태적이잖아! 어떻게 이런 여자랑 엮일 수가 있어?
lines.explanation: twisted = 비뚤어진/뒤틀린(변태적인). get involved with someone = ~와 엮이다/(연인 등으로) 관계를 맺다.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Well, y'know, I had some trouble with it at first too, but the way I look at it is, I get all the good stuff: all the fun, all the talking, all the sex; and none of the responsibility. I mean, this is every guy's fantasy!
lines.korean: 뭐, 나도 처음엔 좀 껄끄러웠는데, 내 식으로 보면 좋은 건 다 챙기는 거야. 재미, 대화, 섹스 다 있고 책임은 하나도 없고. 이건 모든 남자의 판타지라고!
lines.explanation: the way I look at it = 내가 보기엔/내 식으로 보면(자기 생각을 꺼낼 때 쓰는 표현). have trouble with it = 그게 좀 걸리다/받아들이기 힘들다.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, yeah. That is not true. Ross, is this your fantasy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No, of course not! (Thinks) ...Yeah, yeah, it is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: What? So you guys don't mind going out with someone else who's going out with someone else?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I couldn't do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Good for you, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: When I'm with a woman, I need to know that I'm going out with more people than she is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Well, y'know, monogamy can be a, uh, tricky concept. I mean, anthropologically speaking-
lines.korean: 그게, 일부일처제라는 게 좀 까다로운 개념이거든. 인류학적으로 말하자면-
lines.explanation: monogamy = 일부일처제/한 사람만 사귐(반대는 비독점). anthropologically speaking = 인류학적으로 말하면(잘난 척하며 운을 떼는 표현).

lines.text: (They all pretend to fall asleep.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Fine. Fine, alright, now you'll never know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: We're kidding. C'mon, tell us!

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah! C'mon!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Alright. There's a theory, put forth by Richard Leakey-
lines.korean: 좋아. 리처드 리키가 내놓은 이론이 하나 있는데-
lines.explanation: put forth (a theory) = (이론·의견을) 내놓다/제시하다(phrasal). Richard Leakey = 인류 기원을 연구한 유명 고인류학자(실존 인물).

lines.text: (They all fall asleep again.)
[Scene: Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is there as enter except Joey enter.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Tah-daaah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Are we greeting each other this way now? 'Cause I like that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Look! I cleaned! I did the windows, I did the floors... I even used all the attachments on the vacuum, except that little round one with the bristles, I don't know what that's for.
lines.korean: 봐! 나 청소했어! 창문도 닦고, 바닥도 닦고… 청소기 부속도 다 써봤어, 그 빳빳한 털 달린 작고 동그란 거 빼고. 그건 어디 쓰는 건지 모르겠더라.
lines.explanation: attachments = (청소기 등에) 끼우는 부속/노즐. bristles = (솔·브러시의) 빳빳한 털.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh yeah, nobody knows. And we're not supposed to ask.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Well, whaddya think?

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Very clean! It looks great! Terrific!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: ...Oh! I-I see you moved the green ottoman.
lines.korean: …어머, 초록색 오토만 옮겼네.
lines.explanation: ottoman = 등받이·팔걸이 없는 발받침 의자/스툴(소파 앞에 두고 발 올리는 가구).

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Uh-oh...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: How-how did that happen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I dunno.. I-I thought it looked better there. And I- and also, it's an extra seat around the coffee table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Yeah, yeah, it's interesting.. but y'know what? Just for fun, let's see what it looked like in the old spot. (She moves it.) Alright, just to compare. Let's see. Well, it looks good there too. Let's just leave it there for a while.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (to Rachel) I can't believe you tried to move the green ottoman.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Thank God you didn't try to fan out the magazines. I mean, she'll scratch your eyes right out.
lines.korean: 잡지를 부채꼴로 펼쳐놓진 않아서 다행이다. 그랬으면 모니카가 네 눈을 확 할퀴어 뽑았을걸.
lines.explanation: fan out = (부채꼴로) 펼쳐 놓다(phrasal). scratch someone's eyes out = (격노해서) ~의 눈을 할퀴어 뽑다 — 펄펄 뛸 거라는 과장. didn't try to fan out = (그냥 안 한 게 아니라) 펼치려는 시도조차 안 했다 → try to로 '감히 그럴 엄두를 내다'라는 뉘앙스(시도 자체가 선 넘는 짓).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You guys, I am not that bad!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yeah, you are, Monica. Remember when I lived with you? You were like, a little, y'know, (psycho) Ree! Ree! Ree! Ree!
lines.korean: 맞아, 너 그래, 모니카. 나랑 같이 살 때 기억나? 너 좀, 그… (싸이코 흉내) 끽! 끽! 끽! 이랬잖아.
lines.explanation: 'Ree! Ree! Ree!'는 영화 '싸이코(Psycho)'의 샤워 살인 장면에 나오는 날카로운 바이올린 효과음을 흉내 낸 것(=광기·소름).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: That is so unfair!
lines.korean: 정말 너무해!
lines.explanation: That's unfair = 여기선 '공정하지 않다'는 딱딱한 뜻이 아니라 '너무해!/억울해!'라는 감정적 항변. 부당한 취급을 받았다고 느낄 때 자주 씀.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh c'mon! When we were kids, yours was the only Raggedy Ann doll that wasn't raggedy!
lines.korean: 맞아! 우리 어릴 때, 너 래기디 앤 인형만 유일하게 안 너덜너덜했잖아!
lines.explanation: Raggedy Ann = 미국의 대표적인 헝겊 봉제 인형. raggedy = 너덜너덜한/낡은. 'raggedy하지 않은 Raggedy Ann' = 인형 이름과 반대로 깔끔했다는 말장난(모니카가 어릴 때부터 결벽증).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Okay, so I'm responsible, I'm organised. But hey, I can be a kook.
lines.korean: 좋아, 난 책임감 있고 정리정돈 잘해. 그래도 나도 좀 엉뚱할 수 있다고.
lines.explanation: kook = 괴짜/별난 사람(엉뚱한 사람).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Alright, you madcap gal. Try to imagine this. The phone bill arrives, but you don't pay it right away.
lines.korean: 좋아, 이 좌충우돌 아가씨야. 상상해 봐. 전화 요금 고지서가 왔는데, 바로 안 내는 거야.
lines.explanation: madcap = 분별없는/좌충우돌하는(제멋대로 충동적인). gal = 여자/아가씨(구어). (모니카가 절대 안 할 '엉뚱한' 행동을 가정해 놀리는 것)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Why not?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Because you're a kook! Instead you wait until they send you a notice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I could do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay, uh, you let me go grocery shopping, and I buy laundry detergent, but it's not the one with the easy-pour spout.
lines.korean: 좋아, 어, 날 장 보러 보냈는데, 내가 세탁세제를 사 와. 근데 그게 따르기 쉬운 주둥이가 달린 게 아닌 거야.
lines.explanation: spout = (주전자·통의) 따르는 주둥이. easy-pour spout = 따르기 편한 주둥이.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Why would someone do that?! ...One might wonder.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Someone's left a glass on the coffee table. There's no coaster. It's a cold drink, it's a hot day. Little beads of condensation are inching their way closer and closer to the surface of the wood...
lines.korean: 누가 커피 테이블에 잔을 놔뒀어. 컵 받침도 없이. 찬 음료에, 더운 날. 작은 물방울들이 나뭇결 표면을 향해 조금씩, 조금씩 다가가고 있어…
lines.explanation: coaster = 컵 받침. condensation = (찬 표면에 맺히는) 물방울/이슬, 응결. inch (one's way) = 조금씩 나아가다(inch = 1인치씩).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: STOP IT!! ...Oh my God. It's true! Who am I?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Monica? You're Mom.

lines.text: (Monica gasps.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ree! Ree! Ree! Ree! Ree!

lines.text: (Joey enters and he's on the phone.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (on phone) Uh huh.. uh huh... oh my God! Okay! Okay, I'll be there! (He hangs up and to all.) That was my agent. (He tosses and catches the phone.) My agent has just gotten me a job...in the new Al Pacino movie!
lines.korean: (전화에) 어어… 세상에! 그래! 갈게! (끊고 모두에게) 내 에이전트였어. 방금 알 파치노 새 영화에 배역 하나 따줬어!
lines.explanation: agent = (배우의) 에이전트/일을 잡아주는 매니저. Al Pacino = 미국의 전설적인 배우.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh my God! Whoah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Well, what's the part?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Can you believe this? Al Pacino! This guy's the reason I became an actor! "I'm out of order? Pfeeeh. You're out of order! This whole courtroom's out of order!"
lines.korean: 이게 믿겨? 알 파치노라고! 이 사람 때문에 내가 배우가 됐어! "내가 무질서? 당신들이 무질서야! 이 법정 전체가 무질서라고!"
lines.explanation: 조이가 알 파치노 주연 영화('...And Justice for All')의 유명한 대사를 흉내 내는 것. out of order = (법정에서) 질서·규칙 위반; 일반적으로는 '고장 난/잘못된'이라는 뜻.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Seriously, what-what's the part?
lines.korean: 진짜로, 배역이 뭔데?
lines.explanation: seriously = 여기선 '심각하게'가 아니라 '농담 그만하고 진짜로'. 상대가 실없는 소리를 할 때 화제를 되돌리며 진지한 답을 재촉하는 구어.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: "Just when I thought I was out, they pull me back in!"
lines.korean: "겨우 발 뺐다 싶으면, 그놈들이 날 다시 끌어들인다니까!"
lines.explanation: 영화 '대부 3(The Godfather Part III)'의 유명한 대사를 흉내 낸 것. pull (someone) back in = ~를 (도로) 끌어들이다.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: C'mon, seriously, Joey, what's the part?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: ...I'm his (mumbles)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: ..You're, you're 'mah mah mah' what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: ...I'm his butt double. 'Kay? I play Al Pacino's butt. Alright? He goes into the shower, and then- I'm his butt.
lines.korean: …나 엉덩이 대역이야. 알았어? 알 파치노 엉덩이 역을 맡았어. 샤워하러 들어가면, 그때- 그게 내 엉덩이야.
lines.explanation: butt double = (영화) 엉덩이 대역. double = 대역(배우 대신 특정 부위·장면을 찍는 사람; body double = 신체 대역).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (trying not to laugh) Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: C'mon, you guys. This is a real movie, and Al Pacino's in it, and that's big!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh no, it's terrific, it's... it's... y'know, you deserve this, after all your years of struggling, you've finally been able to crack your way into show business.
lines.korean: 아냐, 멋지지, 그… 그… 너 그동안 고생한 끝에 드디어 연예계에 비집고 들어간 거잖아.
lines.explanation: crack (one's way) into ~ = (어렵게) ~에 비집고 들어가다/진입하다. showbusiness(showbiz) = 연예계. (엉덩이 대역인 걸 두고 비꼬는 챈들러)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Okay, okay, fine! Make jokes, I don't care! This is a big break for me!
lines.korean: 됐어, 그래! 놀려라, 상관 안 해! 이건 나한테 엄청난 기회라고!
lines.explanation: big break = (인생을 바꿀) 큰 기회/출세의 발판.
examples: This role was her big break.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You're right, you're right, it is...So you gonna invite us all to the big opening?
lines.korean: 네 말이 맞아, 맞아, 그렇지… 그래서 우리 다 그 성대한 개봉식에 초대해 줄 거야?
lines.explanation: it is = 앞의 'This is a big break'를 받아 '정말 그래/맞아'라고 맞장구(be동사로 짧게 받는 형태). you gonna ~? = are you going to ~?의 구어 축약. the big opening = (영화의) 성대한 개봉/시사회 — 조이가 '엉덩이 대역'이라 opening(개봉 / 엉덩이의 벌어진 틈)을 동시에 노린 이중적 농담.

lines.text: Commercial Break
[Scene: Monica and Rachel's, the next morning, Monica is getting the door.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Alright, alright, alright...

lines.text: (Joey enters with Monica's paper and hands it to her.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Here. I need to borrow some moisturizer.
lines.korean: 여기. 보습제 좀 빌려야겠어.
lines.explanation: moisturizer = 보습제/수분 로션.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: For what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Whaddya think? Today's the big day!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh my God. Okay, go into the bathroom, use whatever you want, just don't ever tell me what you did in there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Thank you! (He goes into the bathroom.)

lines.text: (Chandler enters with the phone.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Where's Joey? His mom's on the phone.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: He's in the bathroom. I don't think you wanna go in there!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: C'mon, we're roommates! (He goes into the bathroom, screams, and runs back out.) My eyes!! My eyes!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I warned you...

lines.text: (Rachel enters from her room.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Who is being loud?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, that would be Monica. Hey, listen, I wanna borrow a coupla things, Aurora spent the night, I really wanna make her breakfast.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, you got the whole night, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yeah, well, I only have twenty minutes until Ethan, so, y'know.. (He starts to raid the fridge.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Ooh, do I sense a little bit of resentment?
lines.korean: 오, 약간 억울함이 느껴지는데?
lines.explanation: resentment = 분함/억울함/앙금(부당하게 여겨 쌓인 불만).

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: No, no resentment, believe me, it's worth it. 'Kay? Y'know how in a relationship, you have these key moments that you know you'll remember for the rest of your life? Well, every- single- second is like that with Aurora.. and I've just wasted about thirty-five of them talking to you people, so, uh.. Monica, can you help me with the door? (He has armloads of stuff.)
lines.korean: 아니, 억울한 거 없어, 진짜 그럴 가치가 있어. 알지? 연애하다 보면 평생 기억할 그런 결정적 순간들 있잖아. 근데 오로라랑은 매 순간이 다 그래… 근데 방금 너희랑 떠드느라 그런 순간 서른다섯 개쯤 날렸으니까, 어… 모니카, 문 좀 열어줄래?
lines.explanation: Y'know how + 절 = '~인 거 있잖아/알잖아'. 여기 how는 '어떻게(방법)'가 아니라 '그러한 상황·사실'을 이끄는 접속사로, 상대도 아는 예를 꺼낼 때 쓰는 회화 표현. worth it = 그럴 만한 가치가 있다.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Sure. Oh, um, Chandler? Y'know, the-the old Monica would-would remind you to scrub that Teflon pan with a plastic brush...But I'm not gonna do that.
lines.korean: 그래. 아, 챈들러? 예전의 모니카라면 그 테플론 팬은 플라스틱 솔로 닦으라고 잔소리했겠지만… 안 할게.
lines.explanation: Teflon pan = 테플론(눌어붙음 방지) 코팅 팬. scrub = 박박 문질러 닦다(금속 솔로 닦으면 코팅이 벗겨져 플라스틱 솔로 닦으라는 것).

lines.text: (She opens the door and he leaves.)
[Scene: A Film Set, Joey is entering for his scene.]

lines.speaker: Director
lines.english: (on phone)...Dammit, hire the girl! (He hangs up the phone.) Okay, everybody ready?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Uh, listen, I just wanna thank you for this great opportunity.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.english: Lose the robe.
lines.korean: 가운 벗어.
lines.explanation: lose the ~ = ~을 치워라/벗어라/없애라(연출·패션에서 흔한 구어, '없애라'는 뜻). robe = (목욕)가운.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Me?

lines.speaker: Director
lines.english: That would work.
lines.korean: 그럼 되겠네.
lines.explanation: That works = 그거면 되다/괜찮다. would를 붙이면 '(그렇게 하면) 그게 딱이겠네'라는 완곡·가정 뉘앙스. 조이가 'Me?(저요?)'라고 되묻자 감독이 시큰둥하게 '그래, 네가 벗으면 딱이지'라고 받는 것.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Right. Okay. Losing the robe. (He takes off the robe.) And the robe is lost.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.english: Okay, everybody, we'd like to get this in one take, please. Let's roll it.. water's working (The shower starts).. and... action.

lines.text: (Joey starts to the shower with a grim, determined look on his face.)

lines.speaker: Director
lines.english: And cut. Hey, Butt Guy, what the hell are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Well, I'm- I'm showering.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.english: No, that was clenching.
lines.korean: 아니, 그건 (엉덩이를) 꽉 조였잖아.
lines.explanation: clench = (근육·주먹·이를) 꽉 조이다/오므리다.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Oh. Well, the way I see it, the guy's upset here, y'know? I mean, his wife's dead, his brother's missing... I think his butt would be angry here.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.english: I think his butt would like to get this shot before lunch. Once again, rolling... water working... and action....and cut. What was that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I was going for quiet desperation. But if you have to ask...
lines.korean: 전 '조용한 절망'을 표현하려던 거였는데요. 근데 굳이 물으셔야 한다면…
lines.explanation: go for ~ = ~을 (연기로) 노리다/표현하려 하다. quiet desperation = 조용한 절망(소로의 유명한 구절에서 온 표현).

lines.text: [Scene: Chandler and Joey's, Aurora and Chandler are in bed in Chandler's room.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: God, I love these fingers...

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: No, actually I meant my fingers. Look at 'em, look at how happy they are.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: (moves Chandler's arm and look at his watch.) Oh my God, I'm late. (She starts to get up.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh no nonononononnononono, don't go.. (He kisses her and pulls her back down.)

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Don't go.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Okay. Oh no, I have to.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (to himself) Look at that, she's leaving.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: (getting up and dressing) I'm sorry. He'll be waiting for me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Well, I thought- I thought you talked to Rick.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: It's not Rick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: What, Ethan? He gets to spend the whole day with you!

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: No, it's-it's Andrew.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I know there'll be many moments in the years to come when I'll regret asking the following question, but- And Andrew is?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: He's... new.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, so what you're saying is you're not completely fulfilled by Rick, Ethan and myself?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: No, that's not exactly what I was..

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Well, y'know, most women would kill for three guys like us.
lines.korean: 있잖아, 대부분의 여자라면 우리 같은 남자 셋을 얻으려고 뭐든 할 거야.
lines.explanation: would kill for ~ = ~을 위해서라면 무엇이든 할 만큼 간절히 원하다.
examples: I'd kill for a cup of coffee right now.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: So what do you want?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: You.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: You have me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Nono, just you.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Whaddyou mean?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Lose the other guys.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: ...Like, ...all of them?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: C'mon, we're great together, why not?

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Why can't we just have what we have now? Why can't we just talk, and laugh, and make love, without feeling obligated to one another... and up until tonight I thought that's what you wanted too.
lines.korean: 왜 지금 이대로면 안 돼? 왜 그냥 얘기하고, 웃고, 사랑을 나누면 안 되는 거야, 서로한테 얽매이는 느낌 없이… 오늘 밤 전까진 너도 그걸 원하는 줄 알았어.
lines.explanation: feel obligated (to someone) = ~에게 의무감을 느끼다/얽매이다(obligation = 의무).

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: ...Well, y'know, part of me wants that, but it's like I'm two guys, y'know? I mean, one guy's going 'Shut up! This is great!' But there's this other guy. Actually it's the same guy that wells up every time that Grinch's heart grows three sizes and breaks that measuring device... And he's saying, y'know, 'This is too hard! Get out! Get out!'
lines.korean: …그게, 내 일부는 그걸 원해, 근데 내 안에 두 사람이 있는 것 같아. 한 명은 '닥치고 있어! 이거 좋잖아!' 하고, 또 한 명은- 사실 그린치의 심장이 세 배로 커져서 측정기를 부수는 장면마다 울컥하는 그 녀석인데- 그 녀석은 '이건 너무 힘들어! 도망쳐! 도망쳐!' 그래.
lines.explanation: the Grinch = 닥터 수스의 동화·영화 '그린치(How the Grinch Stole Christmas)'의 심술쟁이 캐릭터 — 마지막에 심장이 세 배로 커지는 장면이 유명. well up = (눈물이) 그렁그렁 차오르다.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: So... which one of the two guys will you listen to?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I don't know, I-I have to listen to both of them, they don't exactly let each other finish...
lines.korean: 모르겠어, 난 둘 다 들어야 해, 걔네가 딱히 서로 말을 끝맺게 놔두질 않거든…
lines.explanation: let each other finish = 서로 말을 (끝까지) 끝맺게 놔두다 → 부정형이라 '서로 말이 안 끝나게 자꾸 끼어든다'는 뜻. 챈들러 안의 두 자아가 서로 말을 자른다는 농담.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: Which one?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: ...The second guy.

lines.speaker: Aurora
lines.english: (gets up to leave) I see. Well, call me if you change your mind.

lines.text: (She kisses him, he holds her, and kisses her passionately.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Sorry, the first guy runs the lips.

lines.text: (She leaves, Chandler sighs, and falls back on his bed.)
[Scene: Monica and Rachel's, Ross is trying to comfort Chandler. Joey is absent.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Look at it this way: you dumped her. Right? I mean, this woman was unbelievably sexy, and beautiful, intelligent, unattainable... Tell me why you did this again?

lines.text: (Joey enters.)

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hey, waitwait, aren't you the guy that plays the butt in the new Al Pacino movie?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Nope.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No? What happened, big guy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (to Ross) "Big guy?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: It felt like a 'big guy' moment.
lines.korean: 그냥 '친구'라고 부를 만한 순간 같아서.
lines.explanation: big guy = (친근하게) '친구·녀석'을 부르는 호칭. champ/buddy처럼 남자를 다독이거나 격려·위로할 때 쓰며, 살짝 손윗사람이 아랫사람 어르는 듯한(생색·오글거리는) 뉘앙스가 있음. 평소 안 그러던 로스가 갑자기 이렇게 부르자 챈들러가 어색해하는 장면.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I got fired.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Yeah, they said I acted too much with it. I told everybody about this! Now everybody's gonna go to the theatre, expecting to see me, and...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, Joey, you know what, no-one is gonna be able to tell.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: My mom will.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Something so sweet and...disturbing about that.
lines.korean: 거기엔 뭔가 참 따뜻하면서도… 꺼림칙한 구석이 있네.
lines.explanation: disturbing = 마음을 불편하게 하는/거북한·꺼림칙한. (엄마는 알아볼 거라는 조이의 말에 대한 챈들러의 반응)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Y'know, I've done nothing but crappy plays for six years. And I finally get my shot, and I blow it!
lines.korean: 나 6년 동안 형편없는 연극만 했어. 드디어 기회를 잡았는데, 그걸 날려버렸어!
lines.explanation: get one's shot = (드디어) 기회를 잡다. blow it = (기회를) 날려버리다/망치다. crappy = 형편없는(구어).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Maybe this wasn't your shot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Yeah, I mean... I think when it's your shot, y'know, you-you know it's your shot. Did it... feel like your shot..?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Hard to tell, I was naked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: No, I don't think this was your shot. I mean, I don't even think you just get one shot. I really believe big things are gonna happen for you, I do! You've gotta just keep thinking about the day that some kid is gonna run up to his friends and go 'I got the part! I got the part! I'm gonna be Joey Tribbiani's ass!'.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Yeah? That's so nice! (They hug.)

lines.text: (Ross and Chandler look at each other and hug as well.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I'm sorry, Joey. I'm gonna go to bed, guys.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Uh, Mon, you-you gonna leave your shoes out here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (determined) Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Really? Just casually strewn about in that reckless haphazard manner?
lines.korean: 정말? 그렇게 아무렇게나, 무모하게 되는대로 흩어놓은 채로?
lines.explanation: strewn about = 여기저기 흩어져 있는(strew = 흩뿌리다). haphazard = 되는대로의/아무렇게나 한. reckless = 무모한. (모니카가 신발을 안 치우고 자려 하자 레이첼이 일부러 부추기는 말)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Doesn't matter, I'll get 'em tomorrow. Or not. Whenever. (He goes to her room.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: She is a kook.

lines.text: Closing Credits
[Scene: Monica's Bedroom, she's lying in bed wide awake.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (hums for a while, then gives up, and in her head) If it bothers you that much, just go out and get the shoes. No. Don't do this. This is stupid! I don't have to prove anything, I'm gonna go get them...But then everyone will know. Unless I get them, and then wake up really early and put them back! ...I need help! (She buries her head in her pillow.)

lines.text: End
